scopes.title: conversation scope is already operational in the baseline

rules.snippet: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS interaction_turns
rules.reason: conversation turns are persisted as the lowest baseline memory trace

rules.snippet: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS session_context
rules.reason: conversation scope is compacted into a session summary
memory-service records conversation turns through a canonical entrypoint

baseline_read: the baseline already persists raw interaction turns and a derived session summary
this scope is not the current source of a multilayer memory gap

scopes.title: session scope is operational and recoverable

rules.snippet: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS session_continuity
rules.reason: session continuity is persisted as its own scope

rules.snippet: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS continuity_checkpoints
rules.reason: session replay and governed resume are checkpointed
the runtime can recover session continuity explicitly

baseline_read: session scope already exceeds plain chat history because it has continuity, replay and pause resolution
there is no evidence here of a missing multilayer capability for the current cut

scopes.title: mission scope is operational and richer than simple session state

rules.reason: mission state has an explicit canonical contract

rules.snippet: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS mission_states
rules.reason: mission state is persisted independently from session context
the runtime exposes mission continuity as a recoverable object

baseline_read: mission scope is already treated as a first-class continuity artifact
the current gap is not that mission memory is absent, but whether richer cross-scope escalation is needed

scopes.title: user scope is typed and tracked, but not yet runtime rich

rules.reason: user identity is part of the canonical contracts
user_id is persisted in the low-level interaction history
there is still no dedicated user memory contract above ids and turn history
memory-service still has no dedicated runtime entrypoint for user memory

baseline_read: the baseline can track who the user is, but it does not yet recover a richer user scope comparable to session or mission continuity
this supports the hypothesis that user scope is typed but not yet operationally rich

scopes.title: specialist shared memory covers handoff, but not a stronger agent scope

rules.reason: specialist-facing shared memory is formally modeled

rules.snippet: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS specialist_shared_memory
rules.reason: shared memory is persisted per session and specialist
the runtime can assemble and persist core-mediated specialist memory packets
the baseline still does not expose a stronger persistent scope per agent or participant

baseline_read: the current baseline is good at guided handoff, not at proving a richer long-lived agent scope
this supports the hypothesis that shared specialist memory is not the same thing as a stronger agent scope

scopes.title: organization scope is still only a future shape

rules.reason: there is no organization-level identifier in the current canonical contracts
memory registry does not currently expose organization scope as a canonical runtime class
memory-service has no organization memory entrypoint because no canonical consumer depends on it yet

baseline_read: organization scope is absent, but the absence is not yet a proven failure of the runtime
for the current baseline this remains a future shape, not a reopen-ready gap
